gene_description: Interleukin-23 receptor (IL23R) — component of IL-23 signaling pathway; Arg381Gln variant reduces receptor function and mediates response to IL-12/23 biologics in psoriasis and inflammatory bowel disease

source.title: Polymorphisms of IL12B and IL23R genes are associated with psoriasis
source.finding: rs11209026 Gln381 allele (protective variant) showed strong association with psoriasis (OR=1.40, p=3.8×10^-4 for C allele); confers protection from Crohn's disease; molecular basis for IL-23 targeted therapy

CC.effect: Homozygous C carriers (Arg381Arg) have full IL-23 receptor function. Enhanced IL-23 signaling contributes to T-cell mediated inflammation. Strong genetic susceptibility to psoriasis and inflammatory bowel disease. Normal or reduced response to IL-23 targeted biologics in some studies.
CC.recommendation: IL-12/23 inhibitors (ustekinumab, risankizumab, guselkumab) indicated. However, TNF-alpha inhibitors or other biologic classes may offer comparable or better efficacy in this genotype. Discuss treatment options with dermatologist. Monitor response closely at 12-16 weeks and adjust therapy if needed.

CA.effect: Heterozygous C/A carriers show intermediate IL-23R function. Heterozygotes carry one protective allele, reducing but not eliminating IL-23 pathway contribution to psoriasis. Favorable response to IL-23 targeted biologics.
CA.recommendation: IL-12/23 inhibitors are effective first-line biologic choice. Standard dosing of ustekinumab (45-90 mg), risankizumab (150 mg), or guselkumab (100 mg) recommended. Expected response within 12-16 weeks.

AC.effect: Heterozygous A/C carriers show intermediate IL-23R function. Heterozygotes carry one protective allele, reducing but not eliminating IL-23 pathway contribution to psoriasis. Favorable response to IL-23 targeted biologics.
AC.recommendation: IL-12/23 inhibitors are effective first-line biologic choice. Standard dosing of ustekinumab (45-90 mg), risankizumab (150 mg), or guselkumab (100 mg) recommended. Expected response within 12-16 weeks.

AA.effect: Homozygous A carriers (Gln381Gln) have reduced IL-23 receptor function due to Arg381Gln missense mutation. Protective against psoriasis and IBD development. Minimal contribution of IL-23 pathway to disease. Exceptional response to IL-23 targeted biologics.
AA.recommendation: IL-12/23 inhibitors are highly effective in this genotype with excellent response rates. Ustekinumab, risankizumab, and guselkumab all appropriate; expect strong PASI response within 12-16 weeks. This genotype predicts favorable biologic therapy outcomes.
